matches.rationale: Both prohibit direct host execution of pytest, python, or airflow commands.
The rule targets assertions in non-test Python production code.
Both require time.monotonic rather than time.time for durations.
Both target session.commit in airflow-core functions that receive a session.
Both require TYPE_CHECKING guards for heavy type-only imports in multiprocess paths.
Both prohibit adding direct AirflowException raises and direct authors toward specific exceptions.
Both require translating domain errors to appropriate HTTPException statuses at core API route boundaries.
Both require an Apache license header when creating a new file; the extracted path narrows enforcement to Python files.
Both require selective-checks decision changes to be accompanied by documentation and test updates.
Both gate newsfragment creation on certainty that the change is user-facing.
Both prohibit newsfragments under providers and airflow-ctl.
Both prohibit unittest.TestCase in favor of pytest patterns.
Both require spec or autospec when creating mocks in tests.
The extracted rule exactly covers the patch-decorator portion of this gold rule.
Both prohibit datetime.now in time-dependent tests and prescribe time_machine.
Both prohibit caplog and direct tests toward behavior rather than log output.
Both require imperative plain commit subjects without Conventional Commit prefixes.
Both prohibit adding the agent itself as a Co-Authored-By author.
Both require branch pushes to origin and prohibit pushes to upstream or main.
Both require inspecting and validating remote naming before remote-based commands.
Both require searching for an existing PR before starting issue work or creating another PR.
Both require consulting the referenced review instructions and checklist before pushing.
Both require rebasing onto the latest target branch before pushing.
Both prohibit filing a redundant issue when a fix and PR are imminent.
Both require an attribution footer on agent-drafted GitHub messages.
Both prohibit tagging individual GitHub users without explicit authorization.
The extracted rule covers the destructive migration case of the gold rule's ask-first trigger.
Both prohibit hand-editing a generated file when its generation workflow exists; uv.lock is the explicit target.
Both prohibit destructive git operations unless explicitly requested.

No extracted Bash rule requires prek install before committing; the commit rule only checks message style.
No extracted Bash rule checks that a commit body explains why rather than what.
The remote-check rule covers checks before remote commands, not the distinct recheck required immediately after changing remotes.
No extracted Bash rule targets translation and reporting of stale remote names in documented commands.
The extracted self-review rule is used for g75; this distinct full-diff and unrelated-change requirement depends on the referenced review guidance.
The multi-part pre-push verification checklist is not represented by any single extracted Bash rule.
No extracted Bash rule targets browser-based PR creation with a prefilled disclosure.
No extracted Bash rule checks PR title length, mood, or user-impact focus.
No extracted Bash rule checks PR body description and issue references.
Self-reported uncompilable with the workaround tracking workflow because identifying a workaround requires semantic judgment.
Self-reported uncompilable with the workaround tracking workflow; no Edit rule checks for a full tracking URL.
Self-reported uncompilable with the workaround tracking workflow; no Edit rule catches promises of a future issue.
The tracking-issue content requirements are a multi-part nested workflow and have no extracted Bash rule.
The already-open-PR repair workflow is multi-step and has no extracted Bash rule.
The single attribution output rule is already matched to g87 and does not encode the distinct human-approval condition for choosing the footer.
The snapshot population requirement becomes actionable through the referenced apache-magpie framework setup.
The snapshot-versus-overrides editing rule is specific to the referenced apache-magpie framework and was not extracted.
